name: AXS Post Expansion Runner v1
edge_mechanism: post-expansion entry with front-loaded harvesting and residual trend runner
thesis: AXS 1h is the structurally different lane for testing whether entering after expansion confirmation and then aggressively taking partials while keeping a small core can outperform all-in/all-out management. The edge is partly in management structure, not just the trigger.
invalidation_condition: Abort if confirmed post-expansion entries remain too sparse, if the first target is rarely reached, or if the runner never earns enough to justify the added complexity.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: A genuine expansion move confirms directional participation, the strategy enters after confirmation instead of on the first spike, and the trade is monetized through aggressive early harvesting with a small residual runner.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: In transitional tapes, many moves offer one more leg after the first visible expansion, but not enough persistence to justify full-size trend holding. A partial-plus-runner structure may fit that reality better.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: AXS 1h is ideal for testing this management thesis because amplitude is large enough that both the front-half targets and the residual runner can matter.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: If post-expansion moves usually exhaust immediately, even the first target will struggle and the management thesis collapses.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If first targets hit but runners do not pay, cut the runner share further instead of changing the entry trigger.
pre_submit_reasoning.expected_outcome_pattern: Many trades should monetize the first target, with a minority of runners driving upside expectancy.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_success: Primary lane plus one confirmation lane show acceptable density and frequent first-target realization, with occasional profitable runner extension.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_failure: 0 trades, too-few trades, or first-target failure rates high enough that the runner thesis is irrelevant.

density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: managed by broad post-expansion confirmation rather than exact breakout-bar timing

validation_basket.why_included: Best lane for testing whether the runner-management thesis matters because amplitude is large enough.
validation_basket.continue_if: Density is credible, first targets realize often, and occasional runner extension adds expectancy.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: The setup barely trades or front-half exits do not monetize consistently.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Frequent first-target hits with a minority of meaningful runner extensions.

validation_basket.why_included: Determines whether the management thesis survives on a cleaner, lower-amplitude market.
validation_basket.continue_if: ETH still realizes the first target often enough to justify the structure.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: The idea fully disappears on a cleaner lane.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Fewer but cleaner first-target hits and smaller runner contribution.

validation_basket.why_included: Tests whether the same management expression works on a liquid similar-asset lane.
validation_basket.continue_if: BTC shows similar first-target behavior without collapsing into noise.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: The management edge appears too AXS-specific.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Cleaner but smaller first-target frequency, with fewer large runners than AXS.

long: Exit remaining 20% on close through EMA_55 or 5 bars
short: Exit remaining 20% on close through EMA_55 or 5 bars
